codeReview.summary: The feature successfully threads local mode through the CLI and MCP entry points, introduces dynamic provider selection, and persists index metadata. However, two required failure-mode behaviors are still incorrect: `vera search --local` on a non-local build silently degrades to BM25 instead of surfacing the requested compile-time guidance, and the stored `embedding_dim` metadata is not explicitly validated against the active provider on search/update, so dimension mismatches do not reliably raise the mandated re-index error.

issues.description: `execute_search` treats every `create_dynamic_provider(..., is_local)` failure as a reason to fall back to BM25-only search. That includes the explicit non-local-build error from `crates/vera-core/src/embedding/dynamic.rs:51`, so `vera search --local` and MCP `VERA_LOCAL=1` do not return the required graceful error when the binary was compiled without the `local` feature; they silently ignore local mode instead.

The stored `embedding_dim` is only parsed and reused here; it is never validated against the active provider/query dimension. As a result, same-model dimension mismatches degrade into vector-search fallback instead of the required explicit 'please re-index with matching provider' error. The update path has the same omission in `crates/vera-core/src/indexing/update.rs:105-119`, and then reopens the vector store using `config.embedding.max_stored_dim` at lines 188 and 208 rather than the stored metadata dimension.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library has not been updated to document the new env-driven local mode. `VERA_LOCAL` is now part of the feature surface, but the environment and testing docs still describe only API-mode setup.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:185-187` documents local/model-specific indexes, but searching `.factory/library/environment.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` for `VERA_LOCAL` returns no matches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation-worker skill requires manual CLI and MCP verification for a feature like this, but the current handoff/skill-feedback flow allowed the worker to mark the procedure as followed without recording any such evidence. Tightening that shared process would make missing failure-mode coverage easier to catch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:94-106` requires real CLI verification and MCP checks. The handoff for worker session `c63942cb-5aaa-4b9a-972f-ed4e1bcff263` lists only `cargo test --features local`, `cargo test`, `cargo fmt`, and `cargo clippy --features local` under verification while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is `true`.

summary: Fail: the feature wires local mode through CLI/MCP, but two required behaviors are still wrong. Search silently falls back to BM25 when `--local` is used on a non-local build, and embedding-dimension mismatches are not explicitly validated on search/update, so the mandated re-index error is missing.
